vulnerabilities.description: JWT implementation visible but signing method validation incomplete - code is truncated, cannot verify if RS256/ES256 is enforced over 'none' algorithm
vulnerabilities.fix: Add explicit signing method validation to reject 'none' algorithm, prefer asymmetric keys

vulnerabilities.description: No rate limiting visible in validation middleware - could enable brute force attacks on pagination or enumeration
vulnerabilities.fix: Add rate limiting middleware to prevent enumeration attacks

vulnerabilities.description: SAML code truncated - cannot verify XML signature validation prevents XML external entity (XXE) attacks
vulnerabilities.fix: Ensure XML parser disables external entities and validates all signatures

vulnerabilities.description: State parameter uses crypto/rand but code truncated - cannot verify state expiration and proper nonce usage
vulnerabilities.fix: Add state parameter expiration (5-10 minutes) and bind to session

vulnerabilities.description: No input sanitization for SQL injection protection visible - application may use ORM but not confirmed
vulnerabilities.fix: Verify all database queries use parameterized statements or ORM

owasp_checks.detail: Auth middleware present, though full implementation not visible
Argon2id with OWASP parameters for passwords, JWT min 32-char secret enforced
Validation present but SQLi protection not confirmed - no raw query code visible
Proper type definitions and validation middleware visible
Tracing to stdout in dev, but production config not visible
Using current jwt/v5, oauth2, argon2 libraries
OIDC/OAuth2 with state validation, password hashing strong, but code truncated prevents full assessment
No visible code loading from untrusted sources
SAML metadata URL fetching present - validation not fully visible

critical_fixes: Verify complete JWT signing method validation
Confirm rate limiting on auth endpoints
Verify SAML XML parser disables XXE
Confirm state parameter expiration in OIDC
Audit all database queries for parameterized statements
